title: Decaisnina parvifolia
taxonomy: Decaisnina parvifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub native to Australia. The classification of Decaisnina parvifolia places it within the genus Decaisnina, which is part of the Rubiaceae family. Further classification of the species is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Decaisnina, Species: D. parvifolia. This classification is based on its morphological and phylogenetic characteristics.
uses: Decaisnina parvifolia is traditionally used in some cultures for its potential medicinal properties. The plant is said to be used to treat various ailments, including fever and coughs. Some indigenous communities have also used Decaisnina parvifolia to treat skin conditions and wounds.
